name: Roast Fresh Ham, Tudor Style
recipeIngredient: Black caraway seeds (if unavailable, use brown)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim all fat from the ham and discard.
2. Place the ham in a large non-metal container.
3. Mix all ingredients together and pour over the ham. Marinate in the refrigerator for at least 6 hours or up to 8 days, turning several times during marination.
4. Transfer the ham to a heavy roaster pan (enameled iron with lid is ideal).
5. Remove bay leaf, peppercorns, whole allspice and cloves from marinade and pour over the meat.
6. Roast in a 350°F oven, basting frequently. Allow 30-40 minutes per pound; internal temperature should be 165-175°F as measured with a meat thermometer.
7. Remove cover during last half hour of cooking if pan becomes too dry; add additional red wine or water, 1/2 cup at a time.
8. Transfer roast to carving platter and allow to cool slightly before slicing.
9. Serve with baked apples or spiced crab apples if desired.
Hunter's Sauce:
Strain vegetables from roasting pan into food processor. Return liquid to pan and skim off fat. Add 1/2 cup hot water, bring to boil and stir. Reduce sauce to 1 cup, then puree vegetables in processor. Combine reduced cooking liquid and blend until smooth. Thicken with hot water if needed before serving.
